hero.headline: Build faster. Ship smarter.
hero.subheadline: Acme Studio gives your team the tools to design, build, and deploy digital products without the usual friction. One platform. Any stack.

features.title: Instant setup
features.description: Go from zero to deployed in under 5 minutes. No config files, no boilerplate.

features.title: Any stack
features.description: Angular, React, Next.js, Vue, static HTML — pick your target and go.

features.title: Design system included
features.description: SuperCSS ships with every project. Themes, palettes, and components out of the box.

features.title: Mock data ready
features.description: Start building UI without a backend. Real data shapes, realistic content.

features.title: Production-grade auth
features.description: JWT, OAuth, and session patterns ready to wire up.

features.title: Live reload everything
features.description: Frontend and API both hot-reload. No manual restarts.

hero.headline: Built by builders, for builders.
hero.subheadline: We started Acme Studio because starting every project from scratch was wasting the best hours of our week. We fixed that.

team.name: Maya Chen
team.bio: Former engineering lead at two YC startups. Loves developer tooling and bad puns.

team.name: James Okafor
team.bio: Full-stack engineer obsessed with architecture. Builds compilers for fun on weekends.

team.name: Sofia Reyes
team.bio: Design systems enthusiast. Believes good UX is invisible. Built SuperCSS from scratch.

team.name: Daniel Park
team.bio: Open-source contributor. Angular specialist. Maintainer of three npm packages.

team.name: Amara Diallo
team.bio: Writes tutorials, builds demos, runs workshops. Makes hard things feel easy.

team.name: Lucas Webb
team.bio: Keeps everything running. Kubernetes whisperer. Rarely seen without a terminal.

timeline.title: Founded
timeline.description: Maya and James quit their jobs and started building in a San Francisco co-working space with one folding table.

timeline.title: First product shipped
timeline.description: Launched the first version of the template compiler to 50 beta users. Got 4-star reviews and one very detailed bug report.

timeline.title: Seed round closed
timeline.description: $2.4M raised. Hired the first five team members. Moved into a real office (with chairs).

timeline.title: SuperCSS open-sourced
timeline.description: Released the design token system to the public. 800 GitHub stars in the first week.

timeline.title: 1,000 teams milestone
timeline.description: Hit 1,000 active teams across 42 countries. Launched the docs platform and help center.

timeline.title: Series A
timeline.description: $11M raised. Built out the multi-target compiler, added React and Next.js support.

timeline.title: Public beta launched
timeline.description: Opened the platform to everyone. Added the view library, production project system, and the project-to-any compiler.
